blocks.rCode:
mean(x, na.rm = TRUE)
sd(x, na.rm = TRUE)
blocks.pyCode:
import numpy as np
np.mean(x)
np.std(x, ddof=1)

tags: hypothesentests t-test anova korrelation independent paired correlation

blocks.pyCode:
from scipy import stats
stats.ttest_ind(g1, g2)

from scipy import stats
stats.ttest_rel(vorher, nachher)

from scipy import stats
stats.pearsonr(x, y)

tags: regression lineare anova lm ols varianzanalyse fitting models

blocks.rCode:
model <- lm(y ~ x, data = df)
summary(model)
blocks.pyCode:
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
model = smf.ols('y ~ x', data=df).fit()
print(model.summary())

blocks.rCode:
fit <- aov(y ~ gruppe, data = df)
summary(fit)
blocks.pyCode:
import statsmodels.api as sm
import statsmodels.formula.api as smf
model = smf.ols('y ~ gruppe', data=df).fit()
anova_table = sm.stats.anova_lm(model, typ=2)
print(anova_table)

tags: visualisierung diagramme scatterplot boxplot histogram ggplot seaborn matplotlib

blocks.rCode:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(df, aes(x = x, y = y)) + geom_point()
blocks.pyCode:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
sns.scatterplot(data=df, x='x', y='y')
plt.show()

blocks.rCode:
library(ggplot2)
ggplot(df, aes(x = gruppe, y = y)) + geom_boxplot()
blocks.pyCode:
import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
sns.boxplot(data=df, x='gruppe', y='y')
plt.show()

import seaborn as sns
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
sns.histplot(data=df, x='x', bins=30)
plt.show()
